Solomon Cady Hollister papers, 1904-19821915-1982
Collection Number: 16-2-2077

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Solomon Cady Hollister papers, 1904-1982 1915-1982
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
16-2-2077
Abstract:
The papers of Solomon Cady Hollister include correspondence, typescripts, reports, drafts, publications, engineering studies, specifications and computations, blueprints, photographs, tape recordings, slides, and movie films concerning Hollister's work as a civil engineer prior to his academic career.
Creator:
Hollister, Solomon Cady, 1891-
Quanitities:
105.8 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Dean of the College of Engineering, professor of civil engineering.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Solomon Cady Hollister papers, #16-2-2077. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content

The papers of Solomon Cady Hollister include correspondence, typescripts, reports, drafts, publications, engineering studies, specifications and computations, blueprints, photographs, tape recordings, slides, and movie films concerning Hollister's work as a civil engineer prior to his academic career, his affiliations with the American Society of Civil Engineers, the American Society of Mechanical Engineering, the American Welding Society, the American Society for Engineering Education, the American Society for Testing Materials, the American Council on Education, the Engineers' Council for Professional Development and other professional and corporate organizations, and his work as a professional consultant to several companies, including Raymond International, Firestone, Detroit Edison, and Babcock and Wilcox on such projects as the Boulder Dam penstocks; his directorship of the College of the Sibley College of Civil Engineering at Cornell University from 1934 to 1937, and deanship of the College of Engineering from 1937 to 1959, particularly regarding the expansion and development of the curriculum and the engineering campus, and the relationship of the College of Engineering with professional engineering societies and corporations; Hollister's service on government panels and boards of directors, including Raymond International. Files on the Curtiss Wright cadettes.
Scope and content

Also, publications and speeches by Hollister on engineering education, the role of government, and other topics; and Hollister's personal interests, including paleontology, the design of sports equipment, and the study of unusual concrete structures. Correspondents include Bancroft Gherardi, Victor Emanuel, Vladimir Karapetoff, Franklin Olin, Henri S. Sack, Maxwell M. Upson, Alexander M. Beebe, Herbert Hoover, and Fritz Leonhardt. Also information pertaining to the Curtiss-Wright Corporation cadette program.
Scope and content

Commonwealth of Pennsylvania civil engineer's seal for S. C. Hollister.

Ships, Concrete.

Paleontology.
Civil engineering.
Engineering -- Study and teaching.
Concrete.
CONTAINER LIST
Container
Description
Date
Link to the finding aid. PDF file.
Scope and Contents
Not all the material in the collection is covered by this guide.
Series I. Consulting and Academic Work Prior to Cornell
 1909, 1925-1943
Scope and Contents
Articles, reports, engineering studies, computations, and correspondence relating to civil engineering, structures, concrete, and stresses, and to engineering questions interesting to Hollister - such as dust devils, unusual concrete structures, and the Plougastel and Golden Gate Bridges; correspondence regarding the American Society of Civil Engineers and other professional organizations; civil engineering lecture notes; manuscripts and typescripts of articles by Hollister; and some personal material

Box 113 Folder 1
Memos on Building Inspections of Other Universities
 June-Aug 1934, undated
Scope and Contents
Universities visited by R.H. Shreve and H.R. Dowswell; universities include University of Michigan Ann Arbor, Lehigh University, Yale University, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, University of Illinois, Ohio State University, and Purdue University; includes undated handwritten notes

 1934-1937
Scope and Contents
Reports, engineering studies, and data pertaining to civil engineering, welding, and bridges; correspondence with corporations, institutes, and individuals, including the American Society of Civil Engineers, the American Society of Mechanical Engineering, and the American Welding Society; correspondence concerning Hollister's professional consulting; and a small amount of material relating to the School of Civil Engineering at Cornell

Series III. Dean, College of Engineering at Cornell
 1915-1951
Scope and Contents
Correspondence, reports, studies; planning for and construction of Olin, Kimball and Thurston Halls; the College of Engineering budget and inventories; curricula; faculty, staff, students, and enrollment; academic committees; corporate relations; articles, manuscripts, and typescripts concerning research projects, and shortage of engineers

Series IV. Personal Papers and Consulting Records
 1930-1970
Scope and Contents
Correspondence, reports, photographs, specifications, blueprints, test reports; correspondence on Hollister's service on Hoover Commission on Organization of the Federal Government; correspondence with Column Research Council; Hollister's essays, publications, and speeches; Hollister's personal papers include family documents and correspondence; material on investments, inventions, and patents; agencies, organizations, and councils; separate correspondence file of recommendations and evaluations of personnel; correspondents include Alexander M. Beebe, Maxwell Upson, Herbert Hoover, and Fritz Leonhardt

Series V. Blueprints and Visual Material
 1930-1970
Scope and Contents
Blueprints pertaining to College of Engineering and to Hollister's professional consulting practice and personal interests; slides pertaining to Isthmian Canal Studies and other engineering questions; films concerning engineering questions and Cornell Report on Engineering Development; drawings and charts of Cornell buildings; miscellaneous photographs of buildings and laboratories from various periods
